title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
summary:
Luma, an AI video generation startup, has unveiled Innovative Dreams, a new production company in collaboration with Wonder Project, a streaming service dedicated to faith-based content. This partnership aims to produce high-quality religious films and television series, with the inaugural show titled 'The Old Stories: Moses,' featuring renowned British actor Ben Kingsley. The series is set to premiere on Amazon Prime Video this spring, marking a significant step in the intersection of technology and faith-focused entertainment.

Innovative Dreams is designed to revolutionize the production process by integrating Luma's AI technology with the expertise of seasoned filmmakers from director Jon Erwin's team. This collaboration allows creative teams to work in real-time, making adjustments to sets, props, and lighting while incorporating footage of human actors. Luma's newly launched tools, known as Luma Agents, facilitate this end-to-end creative process across various media formats, enhancing the overall production quality and efficiency.

The launch of Innovative Dreams comes at a time when the film industry is increasingly exploring the potential of AI in production. Other startups, such as Higgsfield and Wonder Studios, are also venturing into original content creation, highlighting a growing trend in the industry. As competitors like Runway advocate for a shift in how studios allocate their budgets, Luma's initiative represents a forward-thinking approach that leverages AI not only for cost-effectiveness but also for superior creative outcomes in filmmaking.
social_text: Luma launches Innovative Dreams with Wonder Project, producing 'The Old Stories: Moses' starring Ben Kingsley. This AI-powered studio enhances real-time collaboration, aiming for superior production quality in faith-based content.

title: Thor Explorations 'coming of age' as it moves towards multi-asset growth, says broker
summary:
Thor Explorations Ltd is poised for significant growth as it transitions into a multi-asset producer, according to a recent analysis by Shore Capital. The brokerage has initiated coverage with a 'buy' rating and set a price target of 125p, suggesting a potential upside of approximately 55% from its recent closing price. This optimistic outlook is largely driven by the advancements at the Douta gold project in Senegal and the strong cash flow generated from the Segilola mine in Nigeria. The pre-feasibility study for Douta indicates a clear growth trajectory, with production expected to commence in the 2028 financial year, which will help mitigate the declining output from Segilola.

The Douta project is projected to deliver impressive financial returns, boasting a post-tax net present value of $633 million and an internal rate of return of 61%. Initial production estimates suggest that 411,000 ounces will be mined in the first four years, allowing for the repayment of capital costs of $254 million within just 11 months. Meanwhile, the Segilola mine is anticipated to generate $357 million in free cash flow over the next three years, which will primarily fund the company's development efforts through internal resources.

Management's proven track record, particularly in bringing Nigeria's first large-scale gold mine into production on schedule and repaying project debt within 3.5 years, adds credibility to their growth strategy. Shore Capital also notes that there is potential for further upside through exploration activities in Nigeria, Senegal, and Côte d’Ivoire, with the possibility of establishing three operational mines in West Africa within the next five years, marking a significant milestone in Thor Explorations' evolution as a multi-asset producer.

title: Great Southern (GSBC) Earnings Call Transcript
summary:
Great Southern Bancorp (GSBC) reported its earnings for the second quarter of 2025, highlighting fluctuations in large loan payoffs, with a notable $30 million payoff occurring on the last day of the quarter. The bank's loan portfolio remains robust, with multifamily and commercial real estate lending being the largest categories, totaling $1.58 billion and $1.49 billion, respectively. Additionally, the bank has maintained a strong focus on construction lending, with an outstanding balance of $367 million and an unfunded portion of $644 million as of June 30, 2025. Despite a decrease in total deposits by $73.9 million, or 1.6%, to $4.68 billion, the bank's financial health appears stable.

The annualized net interest margin for GSBC improved to 3.68%, reflecting a 25 basis point increase from the previous year and an 11 basis point rise from the first quarter of 2025. This improvement is attributed to healthy loan yields and effective management of funding costs. However, the gross loans decreased by $157 million, or 3.3%, from the end of the previous year, indicating a cautious approach to loan growth while maintaining appropriate pricing and loan structures. The bank's conservative credit posture and commitment to relationship-based banking continue to be evident in its lending practices.

In addition to net interest income growth, GSBC benefited from significant tax credit partnership income during the quarter. The bank recorded a negative provision for losses on unfunded commitments of $110,000, a notable improvement from the negative provision of $607,000 in the same quarter last year. These results underscore the quality of GSBC's loan portfolio and reflect disciplined expense management. The bank's stable and diversified deposit base further strengthens its financial position, emphasizing its resilience and commitment to long-term shareholder value through prudent risk management.

title: Tesla Just Scored an FSD Win in Europe. What Does That Mean for TSLA Stock?
summary:
Tesla has achieved a significant milestone with the approval of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software in the Netherlands, marking it as the first country in Europe to do so. The Dutch vehicle authority, RDW, has officially endorsed the technology, which allows the vehicle to steer, brake, and accelerate without the driver's hands on the wheel, although the driver remains responsible for the vehicle's operation. This approval is crucial as Europe represents one of Tesla's key markets, where FSD adoption has been minimal until now. The RDW's general manager expressed confidence in the software's safety, even on complex roadways like those in Amsterdam, suggesting that if it meets Dutch standards, it could be suitable for broader European use.

The approval process is set to continue as the Netherlands plans to present its findings to an EU technical committee in May. If a majority of EU member states agree with the findings and the evidence holds up, an implementing act could facilitate EU-wide deployment of Tesla's FSD. This could lead to a rapid rollout across multiple countries, as individual EU nations may choose to adopt the Dutch approval as a reference point, potentially accelerating the adoption of FSD technology across the continent.

However, it is important to note that the European version of FSD will have stricter driver-monitoring requirements compared to its U.S. counterpart. Any significant software updates will need prior clearance from the RDW, ensuring that safety remains a top priority. This development not only enhances Tesla's position in the European market but also sets a precedent for future advancements in autonomous driving technology across the region.
social_text: Tesla's FSD software gains approval in the Netherlands, the first in Europe. This could lead to EU-wide deployment if other countries follow suit, enhancing Tesla's market position significantly.

title: Netflix co-founder and chair Reed Hastings to leave board
summary:
Reed Hastings, co-founder and chairman of Netflix, is set to leave the company's board when his term concludes this summer. This decision, announced in Netflix's first-quarter earnings report, allows Hastings to redirect his focus towards philanthropy and other personal pursuits. His departure marks the end of an era for the streaming giant, which he helped transform from a DVD rental service into a global leader in digital streaming. Hastings expressed gratitude for his time at Netflix, highlighting the joy of enabling global access to entertainment and emphasizing the importance of member satisfaction in the company's culture.

Hastings co-founded Netflix in 1999, revolutionizing the way people consume media. Initially starting as a DVD-by-mail service, Netflix's innovative approach led to the decline of traditional rental stores, including the once-dominant Blockbuster. Under Hastings' leadership, Netflix expanded its offerings to include streaming services, paving the way for a new era in entertainment consumption. His vision not only shaped Netflix but also inspired competitors like Amazon, Disney, and HBO to enter the streaming market.

In his farewell statement, Hastings acknowledged the contributions of CEO Ted Sarandos and co-CEO Greg Peters, emphasizing the collaborative culture he fostered at Netflix. The company recognized Hastings' legacy, stating that his focus on innovation and high performance has left an indelible mark on the organization and the entertainment industry as a whole. As he steps down, Hastings leaves behind a company that has fundamentally changed how audiences engage with media worldwide, ensuring his influence will be felt for years to come.
social_text: Reed Hastings, co-founder of Netflix, will leave the board in June to focus on philanthropy. Under his leadership, Netflix transformed from a DVD service to a streaming giant, influencing the entire entertainment industry.

title: A Single 409A Mistake Can Trigger Taxes on Deferred Pay
summary:
Non-qualified deferred compensation plans are crucial for attracting and retaining top executives, but they come with stringent regulations under IRC Section 409A. This section mandates that executives must make irrevocable decisions regarding the timing of their deferred compensation before it is earned. Failure to comply with these rules can lead to severe tax consequences, including immediate taxation of the deferred amount, a 20% excise tax, and additional interest penalties. Many executives focus on how much to defer rather than understanding the implications of their decisions, which can lead to costly mistakes.

A single error, such as an early distribution or a change in the distribution schedule, can trigger a 409A violation. For instance, if an executive has deferred $150,000 annually for five years, resulting in a plan balance of approximately $800,000, a violation could lead to a staggering tax burden. The immediate tax liability could exceed $462,000, factoring in federal income tax, excise tax, and interest penalties, which can significantly undermine the financial benefits of the deferred compensation plan.

Understanding the core rules of Section 409A is essential for executives to avoid these pitfalls. The law stipulates that distributions can only occur under specific circumstances, such as death, disability, or a change of control. Executives must be diligent in adhering to these regulations to protect their deferred compensation from unexpected tax liabilities and ensure that their long-term financial planning remains intact.
social_text: A single 409A mistake can trigger immediate taxation on deferred compensation, leading to a tax burden of $462,000 on an $800,000 balance. Executives must understand the strict rules to avoid costly violations.

title: Nvidia Stock To $300?
summary:
Nvidia's stock has experienced a significant rally, increasing by nearly 4% to approximately $197 per share, amidst a broader market upswing driven by easing tensions in the U.S.-Iran conflict. Over the past year, Nvidia's stock has surged by 75%, raising questions among investors about its high trailing price-to-earnings ratio of 41x. Despite these concerns, analysts maintain a positive outlook, suggesting that Nvidia's stock could see an upside of up to 50% in the coming years, fueled by robust revenue growth and strategic positioning in the AI market.

The company's revenue trajectory is impressive, with projections indicating a leap from $27 billion in FY'23 to nearly $216 billion by FY'26. Analysts anticipate that by FY'28, Nvidia could achieve revenues of around $480 billion, reflecting strong market confidence in the ongoing transition from experimental AI training to large-scale deployment. This growth is expected to continue, with estimates suggesting revenues could exceed $575 billion by 2029 if the company maintains a growth rate of approximately 20%.

Two primary factors are driving this anticipated growth. First, the shift from AI training to inference represents a significant opportunity, as inference demands continuous computing resources, unlike the periodic costs associated with training. Nvidia's proprietary software ecosystem, CUDA, creates high switching costs for companies that have invested in its infrastructure, solidifying its market position. title: Tax Day is here. How to pocket more of your portfolio's return, according to Bank of America
summary:
As Tax Day approaches, Bank of America emphasizes the importance of tax efficiency in investment portfolios to enhance returns for the upcoming year. The federal deadline for filing income tax returns is April 15, with extensions available until October 15 for those who need more time. However, any taxes owed must be settled by the April deadline. Investors facing significant tax liabilities this year can take proactive steps to minimize their tax burden for 2026, thereby maximizing their overall investment returns. Jared Woodard, an investment strategist at Bank of America, highlights the concept of 'tax alpha,' which refers to the additional returns generated by implementing tax-efficient strategies in investment portfolios. By comparing two portfolios with identical allocations of 60% stocks and 40% bonds, one being tax aware and the other tax insensitive, Woodard reveals that the tax-efficient portfolio yielded a post-tax average annualized return of 7.4%, significantly outperforming the tax-insensitive portfolio's 5.9% return over a 30-year period.

To further enhance tax efficiency, Woodard suggests that investors should prefer share buybacks over dividends when it comes to corporate cash returns. Unlike dividends, which are taxable events for investors, share repurchases do not incur immediate tax liabilities. This distinction can lead to better after-tax returns for investors. However, Woodard cautions that companies may reduce their buyback activities as they allocate more capital towards emerging technologies like artificial intelligence. For those looking to capitalize on buybacks, he recommends the Invesco BuyBack Achievers ETF (PKW) as a viable investment option that focuses on companies with strong buyback programs. By adopting these strategies, investors can effectively navigate the complexities of tax implications and enhance their portfolio's performance in the long run.

In conclusion, as Tax Day serves as a reminder of the financial obligations faced by investors, it also presents an opportunity to reassess and optimize investment strategies for tax efficiency. By understanding the differences between taxable and non-taxable returns, investors can make informed decisions that not only mitigate tax liabilities but also contribute to greater overall returns. With careful planning and strategic adjustments, investors can position themselves to achieve better financial outcomes in the years to come.
social_text: Bank of America reveals that a tax-efficient portfolio can yield a 7.4% annualized return compared to 5.9% for tax-insensitive ones. Consider buybacks over dividends to enhance returns and minimize tax drag.

title: What is a smart contract, and how does it work?
summary:
Smart contracts represent a revolutionary advancement in blockchain technology, enabling automated transactions without the need for intermediaries like banks. These self-executing programs are stored on a blockchain and operate based on predefined conditions. When the specified conditions are met, the smart contract executes the agreed-upon actions automatically, ensuring efficiency and reducing the potential for human error. This automation not only streamlines processes but also enhances transparency, as all transactions are recorded on a public digital ledger accessible to all network participants.

The functionality of smart contracts can be likened to automated workflows that utilize if/then logic. Developers write the code for these contracts using specialized programming languages, clearly outlining the conditions and corresponding actions. For instance, a smart contract can be programmed to transfer assets upon receipt of payment or to release funds on a specific date. This precision in execution ensures that the terms of the contract are met consistently and reliably, fostering trust among parties involved in the transaction.

In real-world applications, smart contracts are increasingly being utilized across various sectors, including finance, supply chain management, and real estate. Their ability to operate independently of human intervention not only reduces costs associated with traditional contract enforcement but also accelerates transaction times. As the adoption of blockchain technology continues to grow, smart contracts are poised to play a pivotal role in transforming how agreements are executed and managed in the digital age.
social_text: Smart contracts automate transactions on blockchain, executing actions when conditions are met. They enhance transparency and efficiency, reducing reliance on intermediaries like banks. Their use is growing across finance, supply chain, and real estate.

title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught

This approach contrasts sharply with traditional robotic training methods, which rely heavily on rote memorization and task-specific data collection.

The implications of π0.7's capabilities are profound. Sergey Levine, co-founder of Physical Intelligence and a professor at UC Berkeley, emphasizes that once robots can remix learned skills in novel ways, their performance can improve exponentially rather than linearly with additional data. This breakthrough suggests that robotic AI may be on the verge of a transformative phase, akin to the advancements seen in large language models, where the integration of knowledge leads to unexpected and enhanced functionalities.

The research team's demonstration of π0.7's ability to operate an air fryer, a device it had minimal exposure to during training, underscores the model's versatility and adaptability. If these findings are validated, they could pave the way for more general-purpose robots capable of understanding and executing a wide range of tasks, significantly broadening the scope of robotic applications in various industries. This advancement could revolutionize how robots are utilized in everyday life and specialized fields alike.
social_text: Physical Intelligence's π0.7 robot brain can perform tasks it wasn't trained for, showcasing compositional generalization. This breakthrough could lead to exponential improvements in robotic capabilities, similar to large language models.

title: Bitwise Says Global Conflict Could Expand Bitcoin Market Beyond Gold: Is That Plausible?

title: Microsoft Takes Over Stargate Norway Data Center From OpenAI
summary:

The deal involves renting 30,000 Nvidia Vera Rubin chips from Nscale, a neocloud provider, at a facility located in Narvik, Norway. This move builds on Microsoft's previous $6.2 billion investment in the same site, underscoring the tech giant's commitment to expanding its cloud computing capabilities, particularly in the realm of artificial intelligence. The strategic acquisition of this data center capacity reflects Microsoft's ongoing efforts to bolster its infrastructure to support AI workloads and services.

OpenAI had initially pursued a partnership with Nscale to utilize the Norwegian site for its own AI operations but ultimately failed to finalize an agreement. This setback comes on the heels of OpenAI's decision to pause its similar Stargate project in the UK, citing high energy costs and regulatory challenges. The contrast between OpenAI's previous ambitious infrastructure plans and its current cautious approach highlights the evolving landscape of AI investments and operational strategies in the face of rising costs.

In a related development, Nscale has successfully secured another client, Google, which will rent capacity at a separate facility in West London. This facility will utilize Nvidia's Grace Blackwell chips, indicating a growing demand for advanced computing resources among major tech players. As the competition for AI infrastructure intensifies, both Microsoft and Google are positioning themselves to capitalize on the burgeoning market, while OpenAI reassesses its strategies in light of financial and operational realities.

title: Adding bitcoin alongside gold to your portfolio juiced returns and didn't raise risk, study shows

Rather than choosing between gold and bitcoin, Citi analysts recommend a diversified approach that includes both assets in small proportions.

Citi's analysis suggests that a 5% allocation to gold can significantly boost portfolio efficiency. By splitting this allocation between gold and bitcoin, investors can further enhance performance, particularly in scenarios where bond markets are under pressure. The study indicates that this strategy is particularly effective in bear-steepening environments, which have become more common since 2020 due to fiscal concerns and rising inflation risk premiums. The analysts believe that the current economic climate, characterized by instability in bond markets and geopolitical tensions, makes this mixed approach even more appealing.

Additionally, the report notes that bitcoin has outperformed gold during periods of market instability, as evidenced by its 9% increase over the past two months, while gold has seen a decline of 4%. This performance underscores the potential for bitcoin to serve as a valuable asset in times of economic uncertainty. As the popularity of spot bitcoin ETFs continues to rise, investors are encouraged to rethink their asset allocations to include both gold and bitcoin, leveraging the unique benefits each can offer in a diversified portfolio.

title: European police email 75,000 people asking them to stop DDoS attacks
summary:
In a significant crackdown on cybercrime, Europol has launched Operation PowerOFF, targeting the growing issue of DDoS-for-hire services. This coordinated effort involved sending warning emails to over 75,000 individuals suspected of engaging in these illicit activities. DDoS attacks, which can incapacitate websites by overwhelming them with traffic, have become increasingly accessible due to these services, allowing even those without technical expertise to launch attacks. The operation highlights the ongoing challenges law enforcement faces in combating cybercrime, particularly as these services proliferate online.

The operation was made possible through extensive investigative work, including the seizure of servers linked to DDoS-for-hire services. By obtaining user information from these servers, Europol was able to identify and reach out to potential offenders. In addition to the warning emails, the operation resulted in four arrests and the takedown of 53 domains associated with these cybercriminal activities. Law enforcement agencies executed 24 search warrants as part of their efforts to dismantle the infrastructure supporting these attacks.

DDoS attacks remain a prevalent threat, with their simplicity and effectiveness making them a popular choice among cybercriminals. The scale of these attacks can be staggering; for instance, Cloudflare reported mitigating a record DDoS attack that peaked at 29.7 terabits per second last year. As law enforcement continues to adapt to the evolving landscape of cyber threats, operations like PowerOFF are crucial in disrupting the cycle of cybercrime and protecting online infrastructure from malicious attacks.
social_text: Europol's Operation PowerOFF targets over 75,000 suspected users of DDoS-for-hire services, resulting in 4 arrests, 53 domains taken down, and 24 search warrants executed, combating the rise of cybercrime.

title: George Kamel Warns Debt Relief Clients: Get Out Now
summary:
George Kamel, a financial expert, recently cautioned clients considering debt relief programs during an episode of The Ramsey Show. He highlighted the pitfalls of these programs, which often charge high fees that significantly diminish any savings achieved through negotiated settlements with creditors. Kamel emphasized that individuals can negotiate directly with creditors themselves, potentially avoiding the costly fees associated with debt settlement companies. He illustrated this point with a real-life example from a caller, Katherine, who discovered that her family's debt relief program would only save them a meager $100 to $200 over the life of their debts, despite the fees incurred.

Kamel's analysis reveals that debt settlement companies typically instruct clients to stop making payments to creditors, which can lead to further credit damage as accounts become delinquent. These companies then collect monthly payments into a dedicated account, waiting until the debts are significantly overdue before negotiating a lump-sum payoff. This process not only prolongs the debt but also negatively impacts the client's credit score, making it a risky strategy for those already in financial distress.

In light of the current economic climate, where the national savings rate is only 4.0% and credit card debt has reached an alarming $1.28 trillion, Kamel's advice serves as a crucial reminder for consumers to be cautious about debt relief options. Instead of relying on third-party companies, individuals are encouraged to take proactive steps in negotiating with creditors directly, which can lead to better financial outcomes without the burden of excessive fees.
social_text: George Kamel warns against debt relief programs that charge high fees, often consuming most of the savings. With credit card debt at $1.28 trillion, negotiating directly with creditors can be a better option.

title: Everything we like is a psyop

The situation highlights a broader conversation about the intersection of music, marketing, and social media influence. As artists navigate the complexities of promoting their work in an era dominated by platforms like TikTok, the line between genuine popularity and manufactured hype becomes increasingly blurred. title: Rogoff Calls Dollar 20% Overvalued, Warns Markets ‘Naive’ on War
summary:
Harvard professor Kenneth Rogoff has raised concerns about the current valuation of the US dollar, suggesting it is at least 20% overvalued. In a recent interview, he highlighted that historical patterns indicate that when major currencies are overvalued, they typically undergo a correction over a five to six-year period. This overvaluation is particularly concerning given the ongoing geopolitical tensions, especially the Iran war, which has led to increased demand for the dollar as a safe haven asset. Investors are currently flocking to the dollar, driven by fears of energy-led inflation and the potential for prolonged price pressures that could hinder the Federal Reserve's ability to ease monetary policy effectively.

Rogoff described the situation as a significant stagflationary shock, exacerbated by tariffs and other economic pressures. He warned that the current market sentiment may be overly optimistic, with investors naively believing that the conflict in Iran will soon be resolved. He emphasized that the reality is likely to be more complex, with further developments expected in the geopolitical landscape. This could lead to sustained high interest rates rather than the anticipated easing, as the Federal Reserve grapples with inflationary pressures.

The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index, which reached an all-time high in September 2022, has since declined by more than 10%. Rogoff's insights serve as a cautionary note for investors, urging them to reconsider their assumptions about the stability of the dollar and the broader economic implications of ongoing global conflicts. As the situation evolves, the potential for a long-term correction in the dollar's value remains a significant concern for market participants.
social_text: Kenneth Rogoff warns the US dollar is 20% overvalued, predicting a long-term correction. He cautions markets are naive about the Iran conflict's resolution, suggesting higher interest rates may persist amid stagflationary pressures.

title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
summary:
Two American citizens, Kejia Wang and Zhenxing Wang, were sentenced to prison for their involvement in a fraudulent scheme that enabled North Korea to exploit U.S. companies through fake IT workers. The U.S. Department of Justice revealed that the scheme, which operated from 2021 to 2024, allowed North Korean nationals to appear as legitimate employees in American firms, ultimately netting the North Korean regime approximately $5 million. The Wangs played crucial roles in managing 'laptop farms' that facilitated this deception, allowing North Korean workers to connect remotely to U.S. companies while masquerading as American employees.

The operation involved the theft of identities from over 80 Americans and the fraudulent employment of these identities at more than 100 U.S. corporations, including Fortune 500 companies. This not only provided North Korean IT workers with salaries but also enabled them to access sensitive information, including trade secrets and source code. The Justice Department emphasized that this scheme posed significant risks to U.S. national security by embedding foreign operatives within American computer systems.

Kejia Wang oversaw the laptop farms, which consisted of hundreds of computers, while Zhenxing Wang hosted laptops at his residence. Together, they created shell companies to manage financial transactions related to the scheme, receiving nearly $700,000 for their roles. The case highlights the ongoing threat posed by North Korean cyber operations and the importance of safeguarding U.S. businesses from such sophisticated infiltration tactics.
social_text: Two Americans sentenced for aiding North Korea's $5M IT worker fraud, exploiting 80+ identities at 100+ U.S. firms, including Fortune 500. National security at risk as fake workers accessed sensitive data.

title: Europe is running out of jet fuel
summary:
The International Energy Agency (IEA) has issued a stark warning regarding Europe's jet fuel supply, indicating that the continent may only have six weeks of fuel remaining. This alarming situation is attributed to escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has disrupted vital oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz. IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol emphasized that this crisis is unprecedented in scale, potentially marking the largest energy crisis in history. The implications of this shortage extend beyond aviation, threatening to impact global economic stability, inflation rates, and overall growth.

As fuel prices surge, the effects are already being felt in the United States, where gasoline has surpassed $4 per gallon and diesel has reached over $5.60. Jet fuel prices in major U.S. cities have skyrocketed from $2.50 to $4.69 per gallon since the onset of the conflict. While the U.S. has some degree of insulation due to its domestic oil production and imports from Canada and Mexico, Europe remains heavily dependent on Middle Eastern oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG). The continent's reliance on refined products from India further complicates the situation, as these supplies hinge on crude oil reaching India from the Middle East.

With the war in Iran showing no signs of resolution, the IEA warns that the situation could lead to significant flight cancellations across Europe. Airlines may soon be forced to reduce operations due to the acute jet fuel shortage, which could have cascading effects on travel, trade, and the broader economy. As the crisis unfolds, stakeholders in the energy and aviation sectors are urged to prepare for potential disruptions and to seek alternative solutions to mitigate the impact of this unprecedented energy challenge.
social_text: Europe faces a severe jet fuel crisis with only six weeks of supply left, driven by the Iran conflict. Prices have surged to $4.69 per gallon, risking flight cancellations and economic instability globally.

These measures are expected to lead to lower transformation costs and improved profitability, as evidenced by Citigroup's return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) reaching 13.1%, the highest since 2021 and above the firm's target range of 10% to 11%.

Mayo's analysis suggests that as Citigroup finalizes its restructuring, there may be further upside potential for the stock. The bank's shares have more than doubled over the past year, reflecting investor confidence in its strategic direction and operational improvements. With the completion of its overhaul, Citigroup aims to enhance its financial performance and solidify its position in the market, making it a compelling investment opportunity for those looking to capitalize on its growth trajectory.
social_text: Citigroup's Q1 earnings soared to $5.8B ($3.06/share), up from $4.1B last year. Revenue jumped 14% to $24.63B, exceeding estimates. Analyst Mike Mayo raised the price target to $160, indicating 24% upside as restructuring nears completion.

title: Google is now targeting bad ads over bad actors
summary:
In its 2025 Ads Safety Report, Google announced a significant increase in its efforts to combat problematic advertisements, blocking a record 8.3 billion ads globally, a sharp rise from 5.1 billion in 2024. This surge in blocked ads is attributed to the enhanced capabilities of Google's AI systems, particularly the Gemini models, which have enabled the company to detect and eliminate policy-violating ads with greater efficiency. Notably, over 99% of these ads were identified and blocked before reaching users, showcasing the effectiveness of Google's proactive approach to ad safety.

Despite the increase in blocked ads, the report indicates a decrease in the suspension of advertiser accounts, suggesting a strategic shift in Google's enforcement methods. Instead of outright banning advertisers, Google is now focusing on blocking individual ads on a case-by-case basis. This change reflects the company's adaptation to the evolving landscape of digital advertising, where scammers are increasingly using generative AI to create deceptive content at scale.

The report also highlights the geographical impact of these enforcement measures, with the U.S. seeing the removal of over 1.7 billion ads and the suspension of 3.3 million accounts. In India, Google's largest market, the company blocked 483.7 million ads, nearly double the previous year's figures, while account suspensions decreased. This comprehensive approach underscores Google's commitment to improving ad safety while navigating the complexities of modern advertising challenges.
social_text: In 2025, Google blocked 8.3 billion ads, up from 5.1 billion in 2024, using AI models that detected over 99% of violations before user exposure. 602 million ads linked to scams were removed, with 3.3 million accounts suspended in the U.S.

title: You’ve heard of hybrid cars. Now meet a hybrid cement plant.
summary:
NOC Energy, a startup focused on transforming industrial processes, has introduced a hybrid heating system designed for cement and glass plants. This innovative technology allows existing fossil fuel-powered facilities to incorporate electric heat, enabling them to reduce reliance on fossil fuels while maintaining operational flexibility. The hybrid system can deliver heat at temperatures up to 1,200˚ C, with plans to reach 1,500˚ C, which are critical for processes like glass production and cement manufacturing. This capability positions NOC as a pioneer in the energy transition, providing companies with a cost-effective solution to electrify their operations without completely abandoning fossil fuels.

The hybridization approach is particularly appealing to companies that are hesitant to fully commit to electrification due to concerns over energy costs. NOC's system allows operators to switch between electric and fossil fuel heating based on electricity prices, optimizing operational costs. The startup has already secured $2.7 million in seed funding, led by 360 Capital, with additional backing from SOSV and Desai VC. This financial support will help NOC further develop its technology and expand its market presence.

As the demand for sustainable industrial solutions grows, NOC's hybrid heating system could revolutionize the cement and glass industries. With few competitors in this niche, such as Electrified Thermal Solutions, NOC is well-positioned to capture market share. The ability to store heat for hours also provides a strategic advantage, allowing companies to utilize electricity during off-peak times when costs are lower, thereby enhancing overall efficiency and sustainability in industrial operations.
social_text: NOC Energy has developed a hybrid heating system for cement and glass plants, achieving temperatures up to 1,200˚ C. The startup raised $2.7M in seed funding, enabling cost-effective electrification while maintaining fossil fuel options.

title: Runway CEO says AI could help Hollywood make 50 films instead of one $100M blockbuster
summary:
Cristóbal Valenzuela, CEO of AI video-generation startup Runway, recently proposed a radical shift in Hollywood's filmmaking approach during an interview at Semafor World Economy. He suggested that instead of investing $100 million in a single blockbuster, studios could allocate the same budget to produce 50 films. This strategy aims to enhance content output and increase the likelihood of creating a successful hit. Valenzuela argues that with advancements in AI technology, studios can maintain the same quality and visual standards while significantly boosting their production volume. This perspective challenges traditional views of filmmaking as an art form, suggesting that the industry could benefit from a more quantitative approach to content creation.

Valenzuela acknowledged the ongoing controversy surrounding AI's role in creative industries, noting that initial skepticism stemmed from fear and misunderstanding. However, he believes that perceptions are shifting as more people recognize the capabilities of AI tools. Runway is actively working with various studios and creators to streamline production processes and reduce costs, demonstrating the practical applications of AI in filmmaking. The company's technology is already being utilized to enhance efficiency and creativity in the industry.

One notable example of this AI-driven filmmaking is the upcoming $70 million movie, "Bitcoin: Killing Satoshi," which is set to be the first studio-quality AI feature film. This project exemplifies how AI can transform the landscape of Hollywood, potentially leading to a new era of filmmaking where quantity and innovation coexist. As the industry adapts to these changes, the implications for content creation, artistic expression, and the future of cinema remain to be seen.

title: Allbirds stock tumbles after nearly 600% rally as the shoemaker rebrands as an AI company
summary:
Allbirds, the sustainable sneaker company, has made a dramatic pivot by rebranding itself as NewBird AI, an artificial intelligence company. This strategic shift comes after a remarkable nearly 600% surge in its stock price, which peaked at $23 before plummeting nearly 30% to $12 per share. The company's market capitalization soared from $21.7 million to $159 million in just a day, highlighting the volatility and investor interest surrounding this transition. The rebranding aims to capitalize on the growing demand for high-performance AI computing, a market that Allbirds believes is currently underserved.

The company plans to raise $50 million to support its new direction, with the funds expected to be finalized by the second quarter of 2026. NewBird AI intends to focus on acquiring high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware and providing long-term leasing arrangements to meet customer demands. This move follows Allbirds' recent sale of its footwear assets to American Exchange Group for $39 million, indicating a complete departure from its original business model.

In its press release, Allbirds emphasized the increasing demand for specialized AI computing resources, citing challenges in GPU procurement and historic lows in North American data center vacancy rates. The company aims to fill the gap in the AI market by offering reliable access to high-performance compute resources, positioning itself as a key player in the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence. This transition marks a significant shift in strategy for Allbirds, moving away from its roots in sustainable footwear to a focus on technology and AI solutions.

title: One AI networking stock has doubled this year. Bank of America says there's more to go
summary:

Bank of America has reaffirmed its buy rating on Ciena, raising its price target from $355 to $550, which suggests a potential upside of nearly 16% from its recent closing price of $475.76. The company is positioned at the forefront of an optical super-cycle, with a substantial backlog of $7 billion and increasing capital expenditures from hyperscalers, indicating a robust demand for network connectivity in the coming years.

Analyst Tal Liani highlights several catalysts fueling this demand, including the need for greater bandwidth and the significant investments in both AI and non-AI data centers. The anticipated massive data center buildout from 2026 to 2028 presents further opportunities for Ciena to scale its operations. Despite its high valuation of over 70 times forward earnings, Liani argues that this is justified by the expected acceleration in growth, particularly as Ciena captures a larger share of the market for networking equipment tailored for artificial intelligence applications.

Looking ahead, Ciena is projected to increase its market share of 800G ZR pluggables from approximately 30% in 2025 to at least 50% in 2026, coinciding with a tenfold growth in the overall market. This trajectory not only underscores Ciena's competitive edge but also positions the company to enhance its cloud market share in optical networking, which currently stands at 50%. As demand for advanced networking solutions continues to rise, Ciena is poised for sustained growth and market leadership in the evolving landscape of AI-driven technologies.

title: This asset management stock is poised for a turnaround as AI, private credit fears prove 'overblown,' JPMorgan says

Despite a nearly 22% drop in Hamilton Lane's shares in 2026 due to concerns about the private credit market and competition from AI-driven financial tools, JPMorgan asserts that these fears are unwarranted. Worthington emphasizes that Hamilton Lane's sales growth outpaces that of its peers, positioning it favorably in a highly valued asset management sector. The upcoming inflows from its largest individual drawdown series further bolster the case for investment.

Overall, JPMorgan's analysis presents a bullish outlook for Hamilton Lane, suggesting that the stock is undervalued given its fundamentals. As the market begins to recognize the company's strengths and the overblown nature of current fears, investors may find a lucrative opportunity in Hamilton Lane's shares. The combination of limited exposure to private credit risks and a robust fee-based revenue model positions Hamilton Lane for a potential rebound in the near future.

title: Meta raises Quest 3 and Quest 3S prices due to RAM shortage
summary:
Meta has announced a price increase for its virtual reality headsets, the Meta Quest 3 and Quest 3S, effective April 19. The price for the Quest 3S (128GB) and Quest 3S (256GB) will rise by $50, bringing their new prices to $349.99 and $449.99, respectively. Additionally, the Meta Quest 3 will see a more significant increase of $100, now priced at $599.99. This decision comes as a direct response to the escalating costs associated with memory chips, a critical component in the production of high-performance VR hardware.

In a blog post, Meta explained that the surge in prices for essential components, particularly memory chips, is affecting various sectors of consumer electronics, including virtual reality. The company emphasized that to maintain the quality of hardware, software, and support expected from the Quest platform, a price adjustment was necessary. This move reflects a broader trend in the tech industry, where several companies are facing similar challenges due to component shortages.

The updated pricing will also extend to refurbished Meta Quest units, although all accessories will remain at their current prices. Meta's decision to raise prices aligns it with other major tech firms like Samsung, Microsoft, and Sony, which have also adjusted their hardware pricing in light of the ongoing RAM shortage. This situation highlights the challenges faced by the tech industry in navigating supply chain disruptions and rising production costs.

title: Canva’s AI assistant can now call various tools to make designs for you
summary:
Canva has unveiled an updated version of its AI assistant, which enhances the design process for users by allowing them to create editable designs through text prompts. This innovative feature enables users to describe their desired outcomes, prompting the AI to utilize various tools to generate multiple design options. The assistant's use of layers provides users with the flexibility to adjust different elements of their designs, ensuring that the final product aligns with their vision. This update is part of Canva's broader strategy to integrate AI more deeply into user workflows, making it a central component of the design process.

As Canva continues to evolve, it is not alone in this endeavor. Competitors like Adobe and Figma are also integrating AI capabilities into their platforms. Adobe recently launched its Firefly AI assistant, which leverages the company's suite of applications to streamline tasks, while Figma has incorporated AI agents to enhance user experience. This competitive landscape highlights the growing importance of AI in design and content creation, as companies strive to provide more efficient and user-friendly tools.

He noted that while many companies are merging workflows, users still prefer to finalize their editing and publishing processes within Canva. By collaborating with major AI players like Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI, Canva aims to enhance its offerings further, allowing users to integrate content generated in other platforms back into Canva for final adjustments and deployment. social_text: Canva's AI assistant now enables users to create editable designs via text prompts, enhancing workflow efficiency. The update allows seamless integration with tools from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI, solidifying Canva's role in design.

title: Fashion retailer Express left customers’ personal data and order details exposed to the internet
summary:
Fashion retailer Express recently addressed a significant security vulnerability on its website that exposed sensitive customer information. This flaw allowed unauthorized access to order confirmation pages, revealing personal details such as names, phone numbers, email addresses, and partial payment card information. The issue was discovered by Rey Bango, a security advocate, who stumbled upon the flaw while investigating a fraudulent transaction on a family member's account. He found that by manipulating the order number in the URL, it was possible to access the details of other customers' orders, highlighting a serious oversight in Express's data protection measures.

The exposed data included not only personal identifiers but also specific order details, including items purchased and shipping addresses. This breach raised concerns about the retailer's commitment to safeguarding customer privacy, especially given its extensive presence across the United States and Latin America. Although Express has since patched the vulnerability, the company has not confirmed whether it will notify affected customers about the incident, leaving many to question the transparency of its response.

Express, now owned by WHP Global, has a reputation to uphold as a major player in the fashion industry. The incident underscores the importance of robust cybersecurity practices, particularly for companies handling sensitive customer data. As online shopping continues to grow, retailers must prioritize the protection of their customers' information to maintain trust and credibility in the marketplace. This incident serves as a reminder of the potential risks associated with e-commerce and the need for continuous vigilance in data security.
social_text: Express fixed a security flaw exposing customer data, including names, emails, and partial card info. Discovered by Rey Bango, the issue allowed access to multiple orders via URL manipulation. Security must be prioritized.

title: This simulation startup wants to be the Cursor for physical AI
summary:
Antioch, a New York-based startup, is making strides in the field of physical AI by developing advanced simulation tools aimed at bridging the 'sim-to-real' gap. This gap represents the challenge faced by roboticists in ensuring that robots trained in virtual environments can effectively operate in the real world. The company aims to create highly realistic virtual replicas of physical spaces, which would allow engineers to program and train robots more efficiently without the need for extensive physical mock-ups. This innovation is crucial as the robotics industry continues to grapple with data scarcity from real-world environments.

Recently, Antioch secured $8.5 million in seed funding, bringing its valuation to $60 million. The funding round was led by A* and Category Ventures, with participation from several other notable investors, including MaC Venture Capital and Abstract. The startup was co-founded by Harry Mellsop and four others, two of whom have a background in founding and selling a security startup to Chainalysis. The team's diverse experience, including roles at Meta Reality Labs and Google DeepMind, positions Antioch well to tackle the complexities of physical AI.

As the demand for robotics solutions grows across various industries, Antioch's simulation tools could play a pivotal role in accelerating the development of autonomous systems. By providing a scalable and realistic training environment, the company aims to enhance the reliability of robots in real-world applications. This funding will enable Antioch to further refine its technology and expand its reach within the burgeoning field of robotics, potentially transforming how engineers approach the programming of physical agents.
social_text: Antioch raises $8.5M in seed funding, achieving a $60M valuation. The startup aims to bridge the sim-to-real gap in robotics, enhancing training for autonomous systems with realistic virtual environments.

title: Kering to purchase minority stake in Chinese luxury brand Icicle

This strategic move is part of a broader partnership with Icicle's parent company, ICCF, aimed at leveraging both companies' strengths. Founded in 1997 in Shanghai, Icicle specializes in ready-to-wear clothing and accessories for both men and women, boasting over 200 retail locations across major cities including Beijing, Paris, and Shanghai. The financial specifics of the deal have not been disclosed, but Kering's investment is expected to bolster Icicle's international expansion and diversify its product offerings.

The partnership is driven by Kering's new initiative, House of Wonders, which focuses on supporting emerging luxury brands. This collaboration is designed to combine ICCF's deep understanding of the Chinese luxury market with Kering's expertise in craftsmanship and brand development. Kering's recent financial performance has shown a decline, particularly in its flagship brand Gucci, which reported a 14% drop in revenue for the first quarter of 2026. This partnership with Icicle is seen as a strategic effort to revitalize growth and adapt to the evolving luxury landscape.

In addition to the Icicle investment, Kering has recently restructured its organization to enhance operational efficiency. The establishment of two new group centers of excellence aims to unify resources and improve collaboration across its luxury houses, including renowned brands like Bottega Veneta and Balenciaga. As Kering navigates a challenging market environment, this partnership with Icicle represents a significant step towards strengthening its position in the competitive luxury sector, particularly in the rapidly growing Chinese market.

title: It's not just gas prices. How the Iran war is coming for your grocery bill.
summary:
The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is significantly impacting the U.S. economy, particularly through rising oil prices that are affecting everyday costs for consumers. As crude oil prices hover around $90 per barrel, a notable increase from $65 earlier this year, gas prices have surged past $4 per gallon, with diesel exceeding $5. This spike in fuel costs is not only felt at the pump but is also rippling through various sectors, including agriculture, where farmers are facing increased expenses for essential inputs like fertilizer.

The American Farm Bureau Federation conducted a survey revealing that approximately 70% of farmers are struggling with fertilizer affordability due to escalating prices linked to rising fuel costs. Fertilizer, a critical component of agricultural production, is heavily influenced by energy prices, particularly natural gas, which is essential for producing nitrogen-based fertilizers. As energy costs rise, so do the production costs for fertilizers, leading to potential shortages and further price increases as the spring planting season approaches.

Experts warn that the geopolitical tensions affecting energy markets can have swift and severe consequences for the agricultural sector. Hunter Swisher, CEO of Phospholutions, emphasizes that fertilizer is a crucial element in the global food system, and disruptions in energy supply chains can quickly translate into higher food prices for consumers. As the situation evolves, both farmers and consumers may need to brace for continued inflationary pressures on food costs, driven by the interconnectedness of energy and agriculture.

The company is targeting a staggering valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion, heavily reliant on Starlink's performance.

The growth is not limited to a single region; both emerging and mature markets are contributing to this expansion. Brazil has emerged as a standout market, with MAUs increasing more than fivefold year-over-year, now accounting for about 13% of Starlink's global user base. Argentina also reported impressive growth, with a 159% increase in users. Together, these two countries represent over 20% of Starlink's active users, showcasing the service's appeal across diverse markets.

In the United States, Starlink's largest market, app downloads surged to a record 1.2 million in the first quarter, tripling year-over-year. This indicates a robust acceleration in subscriber acquisition, further solidifying Starlink's position in a high-growth phase. Analysts emphasize that continued subscriber growth is crucial for attracting public market investors, especially as SpaceX explores new opportunities, including the development of orbital data centers, which could represent the next phase of growth for the company.

title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Devs Propose New Quantum Solution
summary:
In a significant development for the Bitcoin community, a group of developers, led by Casa CTO Jameson Lopp, has proposed a new initiative aimed at addressing the potential threats posed by quantum computing to Bitcoin's security. The proposal, known as BIP-361, was posted on Bitcoin's GitHub and outlines a three-phase plan to mitigate risks associated with quantum-vulnerable addresses. Currently, approximately 34% of all Bitcoin is held in early Pay-to-Public-Key addresses, which are susceptible to attacks from quantum computers utilizing Shor's algorithm. This includes an estimated 1.1 million BTC belonging to Bitcoin's pseudonymous creator, Satoshi Nakamoto, valued at around $74 billion. The urgency of this proposal is underscored by Google's prediction that quantum threats could become viable as early as 2029.

The BIP-361 proposal consists of three phases designed to protect Bitcoin holders. The first phase involves banning new transactions to legacy addresses three years after the activation of BIP-360. Following this, the second phase will freeze all unmigrated coins two years later, effectively locking them until they can be transitioned to more secure addresses. The final phase offers an optional recovery method using zero-knowledge proofs via BIP-39 seed, allowing users to regain access to their funds without compromising security. While Lopp describes the proposal as a rough sketch and suggests that immediate action may not be necessary, the introduction of such solutions indicates a proactive approach to safeguarding Bitcoin against emerging quantum threats.

As the cryptocurrency landscape continues to evolve, the implications of BIP-361 could be profound. If adopted, this initiative would not only protect existing Bitcoin assets but also set a precedent for how the crypto community addresses future technological challenges. The proposal highlights the importance of adaptability in the face of rapid advancements in computing power and reinforces the need for ongoing dialogue within the Bitcoin community regarding security measures. As the quantum clock ticks down to 2029, the urgency for innovative solutions like BIP-361 becomes increasingly clear.
social_text: Jameson Lopp and co-authors propose BIP-361 to freeze quantum-vulnerable Bitcoin, affecting 34% of BTC, including Satoshi's 1.1M BTC. Phased plan aims to secure assets before 2029 quantum threat.

title: Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Hermes luxury sales decline as Iran war hits crucial Middle East demand
summary:
The ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran has significantly impacted the luxury goods sector, traditionally seen as resilient to economic downturns. The war, which began in late February, has led to a notable decline in demand from the Middle East, a key market for luxury brands. The $400 billion luxury sector is experiencing a downturn as high-spending clients from this region reduce their purchases due to the instability and rising costs associated with logistics and materials. Major luxury houses, including LVMH and Kering, have reported disappointing earnings as a direct consequence of these geopolitical tensions.

LVMH, the world's largest luxury conglomerate, reported a 6% decline in Q1 revenue, totaling €19.1 billion, which fell short of analyst expectations. CFO Cécile Cabanis highlighted that the conflict negatively impacted demand in March, contributing to a 1% reduction in growth for the quarter. The fashion and leather goods segment saw a 9% decline, while perfumes and cosmetics dropped by 6%. This downturn reflects a broader trend affecting luxury brands that rely heavily on affluent consumers from the Middle East.

Kering, which owns brands like Gucci and Balenciaga, also faced challenges, with approximately 5% of its retail revenue coming from the Middle East. The region's retail revenue has tumbled, showcasing the interconnectedness of global markets and the vulnerability of luxury brands to geopolitical events. As the conflict continues, the luxury sector may need to adapt its strategies to mitigate the impact of declining demand from this crucial market.

title: Power Insider: The markets are obsessed with the Strait of Hormuz. Why it matters less than you think

However, experts acknowledge the unpredictability of energy markets, especially during wartime, and admit that forecasting prices has become exceedingly challenging. The situation remains precarious, with the threat of conflict looming over shipping routes.

Despite the heightened focus on the Strait of Hormuz, recent developments suggest that its significance to global energy markets may be diminishing. Saudi Arabia and the UAE have strategically invested in alternative pipeline infrastructure, which has effectively halved the volume of oil transported through the Strait. Saudi Arabia boasts a pipeline capacity of 7 million barrels per day, while the UAE contributes an additional 1.5 million barrels per day. This diversification of supply routes has lessened the reliance on the Strait, potentially mitigating the impact of any disruptions.

Moreover, the implications of the Strait's stability extend beyond oil, affecting the availability of essential products such as fertilizers, jet fuel, and helium, which are crucial for various industries, including semiconductor manufacturing. While the situation remains fluid, and the potential for conflict persists, the overall importance of the Strait of Hormuz to global energy dynamics appears to be evolving, with alternative routes providing a buffer against potential shocks in the market.

title: The Rare Earth Trap: How China Outmaneuvered the Entire Western Defense Industry
summary:
China's dominance in the rare earth market is not a mere coincidence but the result of a meticulously crafted strategy spanning over three decades. While the West has largely neglected its rare earth processing capabilities, China has invested heavily in building a monopoly that now controls approximately 90% of global rare earth processing. This strategic foresight has positioned China as a critical player in the supply chain for essential materials used in advanced technologies, including military applications. The recent rare earth shortage has highlighted the vulnerabilities in the Western defense industry, which has become increasingly reliant on Chinese supplies for vital components in jet engines, missile guidance systems, and drones.

The establishment of a new facility by REalloys, aimed at producing 30 tonnes of dysprosium and 15 tonnes of terbium annually, signifies a pivotal shift in the landscape of rare earth processing. This facility represents a proactive response to the looming crisis, as REalloys and its partners have recognized the strategic importance of developing domestic capabilities. Unlike many Western firms that have outsourced processing to cheaper alternatives, REalloys is taking a stand to regain control over a critical segment of the supply chain, thereby reducing dependence on China.

The implications of this shift are profound, particularly in the context of geopolitical tensions. China's willingness to leverage its rare earth monopoly as a bargaining chip during trade negotiations has forced Western governments to reconsider their strategies. The Trump administration's swift response to China's threats during tariff discussions underscores the urgency of addressing the rare earth supply chain. As the global demand for rare earth elements continues to rise, the need for a robust and independent processing capability in the West has never been more critical, making the actions of companies like REalloys a potential game-changer in the industry.
social_text: China controls 90% of global rare earth processing, a strategic advantage built over 30 years. REalloys aims to produce 30 tonnes of dysprosium and 15 tonnes of terbium annually, reducing Western dependence on Chinese supplies.

title: ether.fi’s ETHGas Deal Backs a Bigger Push to Make Ethereum Execution More Predictable
summary:
Ether.fi has made a significant commitment of approximately $3 billion in ETH to ETHGas, marking a pivotal moment in the Ethereum ecosystem. This three-year agreement aims to enhance the predictability of Ethereum execution by establishing a more robust validator base for ETHGas, which is working to create a forward market for Ethereum blockspace. The commitment represents around 40% of ether.fi's total ETH holdings, underscoring the importance of this partnership in addressing the long-standing inefficiencies in Ethereum's market structure. By allowing validators to pre-sell future block inclusion rights, ETHGas aims to move away from the current reliance on last-second spot auctions, which occur every 12 seconds, thereby improving transaction reliability and execution speed.

The collaboration is particularly significant for ether.fi, which manages over 2.8 million staked ETH. By backing ETHGas's model, ether.fi is betting on the increasing importance of predictable execution and forward pricing as Ethereum continues to expand its institutional presence. This strategic move is expected to facilitate a range of applications, including faster DeFi liquidations and optimized trading strategies, ultimately enhancing the overall user experience within the Ethereum network.

ETHGas envisions a future where real-time execution and seamless gas costs become standard expectations for users. The partnership not only aims to improve trading infrastructure but also to create a more efficient Ethereum ecosystem that can support the growing value in stablecoins, DeFi, and tokenized assets. As Ethereum's market continues to evolve, this commitment from ether.fi could play a crucial role in shaping the future of blockchain execution and market dynamics.
social_text: Ether.fi commits $3B in ETH to ETHGas, representing 40% of its holdings, to enhance Ethereum execution predictability. This partnership aims to create a forward market for blockspace, improving transaction reliability and speed.

title: Where’s my tax refund? How to check your refund status in 3 steps.
summary:
In fiscal year 2024, the IRS anticipates that approximately 117.6 million taxpayers will receive refunds totaling around $461.2 billion. This significant amount reflects the overpayments made by taxpayers throughout the year. Understanding when these refunds will be processed is crucial for many individuals who rely on this money for various financial needs. The IRS typically processes most refunds within 21 days of receiving a return, but several factors can influence this timeline, including the method of filing and specific tax credits claimed.

Taxpayers who e-file and choose direct deposit can expect quicker processing times, while those who file paper returns, especially with claims for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC), may experience delays. The IRS has set specific dates for when refunds will be issued, particularly for returns claiming these credits, with some refunds not being processed until after March 2. This creates a need for taxpayers to stay informed about their refund status to manage their finances effectively.

To check the status of a tax refund, taxpayers can follow a simple three-step process. First, they need to gather essential information, including their Social Security number or Individual Taxpayer Identification number. This information is necessary to access the IRS's online tools, which provide updates on refund processing. By staying proactive and informed, taxpayers can better anticipate when their refunds will arrive and plan accordingly for their financial obligations.

title: Bitcoin Halving 2028 Is Now 50% Complete
summary:
The Bitcoin network has reached the halfway point in its current halving cycle, which began in April 2024. With approximately 105,000 blocks remaining until the next halving, expected in April 2028, the block reward will be reduced from 3.125 BTC to 1.5625 BTC. This event is crucial as it directly impacts the supply of Bitcoin entering circulation, which currently stands at around 450 BTC per day. Post-halving, this daily issuance will drop significantly to about 225 BTC, reinforcing Bitcoin's scarcity as a digital asset.

Historically, Bitcoin halvings have been associated with substantial price increases, although the scale of these gains has diminished over time. The first halving in 2012 saw rewards cut from 50 BTC to 25 BTC, while subsequent halvings in 2016 and 2020 reduced rewards to 12.5 BTC and 6.25 BTC, respectively. Each halving has typically resulted in notable price movements occurring 12 to 18 months later, although past performance is not a definitive predictor of future outcomes.

The current halving cycle is particularly noteworthy due to the increasing demand for spot Bitcoin ETFs in the United States, which could influence market dynamics differently than in previous cycles. With over 19.7 million Bitcoin already mined and more than 98% expected to be mined by 2030, the upcoming halving will play a pivotal role in shaping Bitcoin's market landscape and its perceived value as a scarce asset.
social_text: Bitcoin's halving cycle is halfway complete, with 105,000 blocks left until April 2028. Rewards will drop from 3.125 BTC to 1.5625 BTC, impacting daily issuance from 450 BTC to 225 BTC, enhancing scarcity.

title: What is a money order, and how does it work?
summary:
A money order is a secure payment method that allows individuals and businesses to send money without revealing personal information. Unlike checks, which can contain sensitive data, money orders are purchased upfront and can be used in place of cash or electronic transfers. This makes them a popular choice for those who prefer not to use a bank account or want to maintain privacy in their transactions. Money orders can be acquired from various locations, including banks, credit unions, post offices, and grocery stores, making them accessible to a wide audience.

The process of obtaining a money order involves paying the amount of the order plus a small fee, which typically ranges from $1 to $5 depending on the provider. For instance, the United States Postal Service charges between $2.55 and $3.60 for domestic money orders. It's important to note that money orders have purchase limits; for example, USPS limits domestic orders to $1,000 and international orders to $700. This ensures that transactions remain manageable and secure.

While money orders may seem outdated in the age of digital payments, they offer unique advantages such as enhanced security and anonymity. They are particularly useful for individuals who do not have access to traditional banking services or who wish to avoid the risks associated with carrying cash. Understanding how money orders work can help consumers make informed decisions about their payment options, especially for larger transactions or when dealing with unfamiliar recipients.
social_text: Money orders provide a secure payment alternative without sharing personal info. Purchase limits are $1,000 domestically and $700 internationally, with fees ranging from $1 to $5. Ideal for those without bank accounts.

title: When will gas prices go back down?
summary:
Gas prices in the U.S. have surged dramatically, with the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) data revealing a staggering 21.2% month-over-month increase in March, marking the largest rise since the CPI's inception in 1967. The national average for a gallon of regular gasoline has reached $4.118, while diesel prices are nearing $6 per gallon. This spike is largely attributed to ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, which remains largely closed, affecting global oil supply and prices. Despite a temporary ceasefire that has provided a brief positive impact on markets, consumers have yet to experience significant relief at the pump.

The phenomenon known as 'rockets and feathers' describes the rapid increase in gas prices following crude oil price hikes, contrasted with a slower decline when prices fall. David Doherty, head of natural resources research at BloombergNEF, explains that while crude oil prices can influence gasoline prices quickly, the reverse is not true. Factors such as refining costs, distribution disruptions, and retailer markups contribute to the sluggish decrease in gas prices after a spike. It typically takes about three weeks for changes in crude prices to fully reflect at the pump, creating a lag that can frustrate consumers.

Looking ahead, the market remains on edge regarding the potential for renewed conflict in the Gulf region. Should the ceasefire collapse, experts predict that gas prices could spike again rapidly, with current oil prices hovering around $95-$100 per barrel. The uncertainty surrounding geopolitical developments continues to weigh heavily on both consumers and the broader market, making it difficult to predict when gas prices might stabilize or decline significantly. As the situation evolves, drivers are left to navigate the financial strain of elevated fuel costs.
social_text: Gas prices soared 21.2% in March, hitting $4.118/gallon for regular gas, the largest increase since 1967. Diesel approaches $6. Market anxiety persists as tensions in the Middle East continue to impact oil supply.

title: Yield discipline, fuel price surge driving LTL rates to new highs in Q2
summary:
In the first quarter of the year, trucking rate indexes experienced a significant surge, primarily driven by rising diesel fuel prices and a decline in truckload capacity. A recent quarterly report from 3PL AFS Logistics and financial services firm TD Cowen highlights that these trends are likely to strain shippers' budgets as the freight cycle shifts. The less-than-truckload (LTL) rates have remained elevated, with the LTL rate-per-pound component of the TD Cowen-AFS Freight Index showing a remarkable 66.9% increase compared to its January 2018 baseline. Although this figure is slightly lower than the previous quarter, it still represents a substantial year-over-year increase of 300 basis points.

Looking ahead to the second quarter, projections indicate that the dataset will rise to 68.4% above the baseline, marking a new high and continuing a streak of ten consecutive year-over-year increases. The weight per shipment has also seen a notable increase of 3.8% from the fourth quarter, marking the first sequential rise in two years. This uptick is particularly significant as the industrial sector, which constitutes nearly two-thirds of LTL revenue, appears to be recovering from a prolonged downturn. The Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) for manufacturing remained in expansion territory throughout the first quarter, although concerns regarding tariffs and geopolitical tensions persist.

As higher shipment weights and fuel prices continue to influence the market, the cost per shipment in the LTL sector has risen by 3% sequentially, keeping it over 40% above the baseline. Mich Fabriga, vice president of LTL pricing at AFS Logistics, noted that the stability of LTL pricing has depended on carriers avoiding pricing concessions to maintain volume during a lengthy demand trough. With fuel prices expected to have a lasting impact, there are finally signs of recovery in demand, suggesting a potential shift in the dynamics of the LTL market moving forward.
social_text: Trucking rates surged in Q1, driven by a 66.9% rise in LTL rates and a 3% increase in cost per shipment. Projections for Q2 show a 68.4% increase, marking ten consecutive year-over-year gains amid rising fuel prices.

title: Oil shock 2.0: Inflation back, rate cuts fading
summary:
The recent surge in oil prices has triggered a significant economic response, marking what analysts are calling 'Oil Shock 2.0'. Unlike previous spikes, this situation is characterized by a severe supply shock, with the International Energy Agency reporting a loss of approximately 1.5 million barrels per day from the market. This disruption is not merely a temporary fluctuation; it is reshaping inflation, interest rates, and growth expectations in a more persistent manner than seen in 2022. The current oil price has fluctuated dramatically, reaching near $150 before stabilizing around $100, indicating a volatile market that is affecting global economic momentum.

The implications of this oil shock are profound, as inflation rates are no longer cooling but are instead reaccelerating. The OECD has projected that inflation could rise toward 4% as energy costs permeate through the economy. This is particularly concerning given that even a modest increase in oil prices can significantly impact the Consumer Price Index (CPI) within a short timeframe. As a result, central banks are facing a critical dilemma: they must choose between combating inflation and risking a slowdown in growth or supporting growth at the potential cost of reigniting inflation.

The Federal Reserve and other central banks are now reassessing their monetary policies in light of these developments. With rate cuts being pushed further into the future, the financial markets are adjusting their expectations accordingly. Policymakers are aware that energy-driven inflation poses unique challenges, as traditional monetary policy tools may not effectively address the underlying issues caused by supply constraints. As the situation evolves, the economic landscape is likely to remain turbulent, with significant implications for both consumers and businesses alike.
social_text: The International Energy Agency reports a historic oil supply shock, with 1.5 million barrels per day lost. Inflation is set to rise toward 4%, complicating central banks' decisions on rate cuts, now pushed to 2026.

title: Meta extends custom chips deal with Broadcom to power AI ambitions

Broadcom's CEO, Hock Tan, will transition from a board member at Meta to an advisory role focused on the custom chip strategy. This shift indicates a deepening of the partnership, as both companies aim to leverage Broadcom's expertise in chip design and infrastructure software. Meta's CEO, Mark Zuckerberg, emphasized the importance of this collaboration in building a robust computing foundation necessary for delivering advanced AI capabilities to billions of users.

The first chip from Meta's Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) program, known as the MTIA 300, is already operational, enhancing Meta's ranking and recommendation systems. The roadmap includes three additional chip generations expected to launch by 2027, with a focus on inference capabilities that allow AI models to effectively respond to user queries. This partnership not only positions Meta to compete more effectively in the AI space but also highlights Broadcom's growing significance in the generative AI market, as evidenced by the recent uptick in its stock performance following the announcement.
